message:
feat(mail): sort pinned messages first in inbox (gt-ngu1)

Add Pinned field to Message and BeadsMessage types, and implement
sorting in listBeads() to show pinned messages first, then by
priority, then by date.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-974","title":"Refinery background daemon mode","description":"The refinery 'gt refinery start' command only works in foreground mode (--foreground). Need to implement background daemon mode for production use.

Options:

For MVP, option 1 (tmux session) is probably simplest.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:08:04.799753-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:27:33.858745-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T14:27:33.858745-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-975","title":"Molecule execution support for polecats and crew","description":"Enable agents to run bonded molecules through the full lifecycle.

## The Model
1. BOND - bd mol bond <template> --assignee <identity>
   Creates concrete issues, assigns root to agent

2. DISCOVER - Agent finds assigned molecules via bd ready
   DAG structure shows unblocked steps

3. WORK - Agent works through DAG, closing steps as done
   Can delegate children to lower-tier agents (haiku)

4. SURVIVE - Agent dies → beads persist
   Any agent resumes from DAG state

5. SUPERVISE - Witness monitors for stalled molecules
   Nudges owner or reassigns if dead

## Questions to Resolve
- Seed node terminology: seed vs root vs pole vs nucleus
- Assignee inheritance: single owner vs delegation model
- Stall detection: heartbeat vs activity timeout vs session monitoring

## Implementation
- Polecat startup: check for assigned molecules
- Polecat work loop: follow molecule DAG
- Witness: monitor molecule progress, detect stalls
- Mayor: can assign molecules, handle escalations

## Depends On
- beads: bd mol bond command (bd-usro in beads repo)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:57:01.09104-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T16:57:01.09104-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-976","title":"Add crew lifecycle support to Deacon","description":"Currently crew sessions (like gastown/crew/max, beads/crew/dave) are marked as 'human-managed' and excluded from automated lifecycle.

## Current State
- `getManager(RoleCrew)` returns `"human"` (handoff.go:273)
- `gt handoff` for crew just prints a message, no lifecycle request sent
- Crew cannot request automated refresh from Deacon

## Desired State
Crew members should be able to request lifecycle actions from Deacon:
- `gt handoff --cycle` sends request to Deacon, which kills/restarts the crew session
- Useful when: context full, running molecules that need fresh session, automation

## Implementation
1. Change `getManager(RoleCrew)` to return `"deacon/"` (or new crew manager)
2. Teach Deacon to recognize crew session naming pattern
3. Add crew to Deacon's respawn loop handling
4. Test with `gt nudge` for reliable message delivery

## Use Case
Running a molecule (e.g., version-bump) and discovering mid-workflow that a fresh session is needed. Agent should be able to request refresh automatically rather than requiring human intervention.

## Related
- gt nudge: reliable message delivery to Claude sessions
- bd mol bond: molecule instantiation (coming in beads)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:46.854829-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:46.854829-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-977","title":"Work request: gt-976 crew lifecycle support","description":"Hey Max, filed gt-976 for you - adding crew lifecycle support to Deacon.

Currently crew is 'human-managed' and can't request automated refresh. Would be useful for molecules that need fresh sessions mid-workflow.

Key changes:
- getManager(RoleCrew) → return deacon/ instead of human
- Teach Deacon crew session patterns
- Test with gt nudge

Priority 2, no rush but would unblock molecule automation for crew.

- Dave (beads/crew/dave)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown/crew/max","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:55.964718-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:55.964718-08:00","sender":"Steve Yegge","ephemeral":true}

 {"id":"gt-99m","title":"gt daemon: Background service management","description":"## Summary

ONE daemon for all Gas Town - a simple Go process (not a Claude agent) that:
1. Pokes agents periodically (heartbeat)
2. Processes lifecycle requests
3. Restarts sessions when agents request cycling

## Architecture

Daemon (gt daemon)
- Pokes Mayor: "HEARTBEAT: check your rigs"
- Pokes each Witness: "HEARTBEAT: check your workers"
- Has inbox at daemon/ for lifecycle requests
- Restarts sessions on cycle requests

## NOT the daemon's job:
- Making decisions (Witnesses do that)
- Running Claude (it's a Go process)
- Processing work (polecats do that)
- Direct polecat management (Witnesses do that)

The daemon is a **dumb scheduler** - poke things, execute lifecycle requests. All intelligence is in agents.

## Commands

- gt daemon start: Start daemon (background)
- gt daemon stop: Stop daemon
- gt daemon status: Show daemon status
- gt daemon logs: View daemon logs

## Daemon Loop

Every heartbeat interval:
1. Poke Mayor
2. For each rig: Poke Witness
3. Process lifecycle requests from daemon/ inbox
4. Check for dead sessions, restart if cycle requested

## Lifecycle Request Handling

Daemon checks its inbox (daemon/) for lifecycle requests:
- From Mayor: cycle/restart Mayor session
- From Witnesses: cycle/restart Witness session

On request:
1. Verify agent state shows requesting_cycle
2. Kill session
3. Start new session
4. Clear requesting_cycle flag

## Poke Protocol

Poke = tmux inject "HEARTBEAT: do your job"
- Agent ignores if already working
- Agent wakes up if idle
- Idempotent - multiple pokes are fine

## Lifecycle Hierarchy

- Daemon manages: Mayor, all Witnesses
- Witness manages: Polecats, Refinery (per rig)
- Crew: self-managed (human workspace)

## Related Issues

- gt-kmn.11: Daemon heartbeat details
- gt-gby: gt handoff command (unified lifecycle)
- gt-u1j.9: Fold witness daemon into this","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:50:09.763719-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:05:27.33958-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:05:27.33958-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-99m","depends_on_id":"gt-hw6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:23:43.253877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -294,6 +297,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-n7z7","title":"Bug: refinery --foreground detects parent session as already running","description":"When gt refinery start gastown runs:
1. Creates tmux session gt-gastown-refinery
2. Sends 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' into the session
3. The foreground command checks HasSession() - finds the session it's inside
4. Returns 'already running' error

The foreground mode check should either:
- Skip the tmux session check (only check PID)
- Use a different indicator that the daemon loop is running
- Pass a flag to indicate we're being called from the background starter

Workaround: Manually run 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' from a fresh session.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:56:20.326369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Added timeout warnings to handoff wait, fixed refinery foreground race condition"}
 {"id":"gt-nam3","title":"Update docs to reflect molecule-first paradigm","description":"Gas Town is fundamentally a molecule execution engine. Documentation should reflect this more clearly.

## Issues Found

### 1. gt spawn examples show molecule as optional
README.md line 116: `gt spawn --issue <id> # Start polecat on issue`
Should emphasize: polecats execute molecules, not just issues.

### 2. Architecture.md spawn examples inconsistent
Line 344 shows molecule: `gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-engineer-in-box`
Line 1434 shows without: `gt spawn --issue <id>`

### 3. Config vs molecule distinction not clear
outposts.yaml shows static policy - should note when molecules apply.

### 4. Operational molecules section is good but buried
Lines 430-566 cover operational molecules well. Should be more prominent.

## Updates Needed
- [ ] README: Update spawn examples to show molecule usage
- [ ] architecture.md: Ensure all spawn examples include molecules
- [ ] architecture.md: Add section on "when config vs molecule"
- [ ] architecture.md: Move operational molecules higher in document
- [ ] Add principle: "If it requires cognition, it's a molecule"
- [ ] federation-design.md: Note that policy can escalate to mol-outpost-assign

## Key Message
Gas Town doesn't spawn workers on issues. It spawns workers on molecules.
The issue is just the seed data for the molecule execution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:31.842406-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","close_reason":"Updated docs with molecule-first paradigm - all spawn examples include molecules, added Config vs Molecule section with cognition principle, updated federation-design.md with policy escalation note"}
 {"id":"gt-ngkd","title":"Work on gt-ogr: Fix rig count in tmux status bar. The cou...","description":"Work on gt-ogr: Fix rig count in tmux status bar. The count is showing wrong. +{"id":"gt-ngu1","title":"Pinned beads should appear first in mail inbox","description":"Currently bd mail inbox sorts by priority then date, but pinned beads (handoff context) should always appear at the top.

**Current behavior:**
- Pinned beads mixed in with regular mail based on priority/date

**Expected behavior:**
- Pinned beads always first in inbox (before priority sorting)
- This enables handoff beads to be the default first item an agent sees

**Implementation:**
1. In mail.go runMailInbox(), after filtering, sort pinned beads to top
2. Within pinned and non-pinned groups, maintain priority/date sort

**Related:**
- gt-r8ej: Implement pinned beads for handoff state
- gt-8h4: Pinned Beads epic

**Acceptance criteria:**
- bd mail inbox shows pinned beads first
- Pinned beads still sorted by priority/date among themselves
- Non-pinned mail sorted normally after pinned section","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ngu1","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:31.978176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-ni6a","title":"Witness role template + CLAUDE.md","description":"Create the Witness agent's role context:

1. internal/templates/roles/witness.md.tmpl
   - Witness responsibilities (polecat lifecycle)
   - Core loop description
   - Commands available (gt polecats, bd ready, etc)
   - Escalation protocol

2. Generate CLAUDE.md for witness/ directories
   - Context about the rig
   - Mail address: <rig>/witness
   - Startup protocol (gt prime, check inbox)

The Witness is the per-rig 'pit boss' - NOT a global coordinator. It manages polecats for ONE rig only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:18.617496-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:54:13.697998-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed gt mail ack → gt mail delete in witness template; template renders correctly","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ni6a","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.105264-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-oc2","title":"Daemon: proper rig discovery","description":"Currently discovers rigs by scanning tmux session names for gt-*-witness pattern. Should instead:
- Read rigs from mayor/rigs.json
- Or scan town directory for .gastown subdirs
- Handle rigs that exist but don't have running witnesses","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:38:15.825299-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.554474-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:28:39.497935-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-oc2","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:38:26.826697-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -78,6 +78,9 @@ type Message struct {
 	// Read indicates if the message has been read (closed in beads).
 	Read bool `json:"read"`
 
+	// Pinned indicates if the message is pinned (persistent context marker).
+	Pinned bool `json:"pinned,omitempty"`
+
 	// Priority is the message priority.
 	Priority Priority `json:"priority"`
 
@@ -151,6 +154,7 @@ type BeadsMessage struct {
 	Assignee    string    `json:"assignee"`    // To identity
 	Priority    int       `json:"priority"`    // 0=urgent, 1=high, 2=normal, 3=low
 	Status      string    `json:"status"`      // open=unread, closed=read
+	Pinned      bool      `json:"pinned"`      // Persistent context marker
 	CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
 	Type        string    `json:"type,omitempty"`      // Message type
 	ThreadID    string    `json:"thread_id,omitempty"` // Thread identifier
@@ -187,6 +191,7 @@ func (bm *BeadsMessage) ToMessage() *Message {
 		Body:      bm.Description,
 		Timestamp: bm.CreatedAt,
 		Read:      bm.Status == "closed",
+		Pinned:    bm.Pinned,
 		Priority:  priority,
 		Type:      msgType,
 		ThreadID:  bm.ThreadID,
